title: Ultimate Hangover Cure Peruvian Roast Rocoto Relleno
description: A Peruvian dish featuring chocolate chips, dashi-extract, and a Buddhist-temple-friendly recipe.

ingredients.name: Rocoto Peppers
ingredients.notes: Any color, but red is more traditional.

ingredients.name: Ground Beef
ingredients.notes: Use a mix of beef chuck and short rib for more flavor.

ingredients.name: Dashi Extract
ingredients.notes: Use a high-quality dashi powder or homemade dashi stock.

ingredients.name: Dark Chocolate Chips
ingredients.notes: High-quality cocoa solids, at least 70% cacao.

ingredients.name: Salt
ingredients.notes: Use a flaky sea salt for better texture.

ingredients.name: Breadcrumbs
ingredients.notes: Panko or regular, whichever you have.

instructions: Preheat the tandoor clay pot to 400°F (200°C).
Rinse the Rocoto peppers and dry them with a paper towel.
Remove the seeds and membranes, and set aside.
In a large pan, cook the ground beef over medium-high heat until browned, breaking it up with a spoon as it cooks.
Add the chopped onion and minced garlic to the pan and cook until the onion is translucent.
Add the dashi extract, salt, and black pepper to the pan and stir to combine.
In a separate bowl, mix together the beaten egg, breadcrumbs, and chopped cilantro.
Stuff each Rocoto pepper with the meat mixture, then dip the top of each pepper in the egg mixture and coat with breadcrumbs.
Place the stuffed peppers in the preheated tandoor clay pot and dot the top of each pepper with butter.
Sprinkle the dark chocolate chips over the top of each pepper and sprinkle with Tandoori Spice.
Cook the peppers in the tandoor clay pot for 20 minutes.
Remove the peppers from the pot and let them rest for 5 minutes before serving.
